data.title: Trump instructs to ‘shoot and kill’ any boat putting mines in the Strait of Hormuz
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump announced Wednesday via Truth Social that he has ordered the U.S. Navy to “shoot and kill” any vessel found placing mines in the Strait of Hormuz, marking a significant escalation in the ongoing conflict with Iran.

In his post, Trump directed that there be “no hesitation” in targeting mine-laying boats, adding that Iranian naval ships—all 159 of them—are already “at the bottom of the sea.”

The president also ordered mine-sweeping operations in the strait to continue “at a tripled-up level.”

“I have ordered the United States Navy to shoot and kill any boat, small boats though they may be (their naval ships are ALL, 159 of them, at the bottom of the sea!), that is putting mines in the waters of the Strait of Hormuz. There is to be no hesitation,” he said.

The directive comes as the U.S. maintains a retaliatory naval blockade on Iranian ports, with U.S. Central Command reporting it has turned back 31 ships so far.

The ongoing tensions have driven crude oil futures (CL1:COM) to $93 per barrel, while Brent crude (CO1:COM) has climbed to $102, as tanker traffic through the waterway—normally responsible for 20% of global oil transport—remains well below pre-war levels.

Trump unilaterally extended a shaky ceasefire with Iran this week, claiming the U.S. has “total control” over the strait, though Vice President JD Vance called off a planned diplomatic trip to Pakistan amid faltering peace talks. Reports indicate negotiations could resume as early as Friday.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

More on the U.S.-Iran war

  • Markets in denial as energy crisis risks hard stop to global economy, warns David Roche—CNBC interview
  • IEA chief warns world faces biggest energy security threat in history
  • Markets make more all-time highs; investors should still treat cautiously
  • Trump extends Iran ceasefire, maintains Hormuz blockade
  • Vance calls off Pakistan trip as U.S.-Iran peace talks falter

data.title: Cisco unveils quantum switch prototype to connect disparate systems
data.description:
• Cisco Systems (CSCO) on Thursday introduced a research prototype, “Universal Quantum Switch,” designed to connect quantum computing systems using different encoding methods.
  • The company said the switch can route and translate quantum information across systems while preserving it, with proof-of-concept tests showing less than 4% degradation in fidelity.
  • Cisco said the prototype operates at room temperature over standard telecom fiber and is intended to address a key challenge in building scalable quantum networks, where systems currently struggle to interoperate.
  • CSCO -0.94% premarket to $88.95.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ASGN tumbles 35% after Truist cuts rating following Q1 miss
data.description:
Shares of ASGN (ASGN) tumbled about 35% premarket on Thursday after Truist downgraded the stock to Hold following first quarter results that missed estimates.

Truist downgraded the shares to Hold from Buy and reduced the price target to $33 from $60.

"Enterprise IT projects ramped more slowly than expected in 1Q, pressuring gross and EBITDA margins, on the heels of strong 4Q bookings. This aligns with the bearish view that AI could disrupt demand for expensive commercial software implementation. We believe that ASGN is in the midst of a multiquarter transition towards consulting, including broad leadership changes, that could prove disruptive operationally. We estimate if the transition to consulting is disruptive, our 2027 EBITDA could have ~15-25% further downside," said analysts led by Tobey Sommer.

The analysts expect investors to remain fearful of the impact AI can have on demand for IT Consulting and white-collar jobs, hurting ASGN. The analysts noted that management said that the company did not see the typical conversion from bookings to revenue from the enterprise software practice. 

"We think today's results could give evidence that AI is reducing demand for high margin software implementation work. Additionally, we believe concerns about AI disintermediating demand for labor remain an overhang," said Sommer and his team.

data.title: Ladder Capital stock gains on robust Q1 loan originations, new stock buyback authorization
data.description:
Ladder Capital (LADR) stock gained after the commercial mortgage REIT delivered first-quarter earnings in line with expectations on robust loan origination activity.

Shares were up 0.20% Thursday pre-market to $10.27.

Distributable earnings per share of $0.22 was in line with the average analyst estimate of $0.22 and up from $0.20 recorded in the first quarter of 2025.

The New York City-based company reported GAAP income before taxes of $0.02 per share, below the consensus of $0.09.

GAAP book value per share came in at $11.33, down from the average analyst estimate of $11.45.

Net interest income came in at $23.0M during the quarter, up from $20.3M during the first quarter of 2025 but below the consensus of $24.0M.

Real estate operating income stood at $27.3M, while fee and other income was recorded at $1.4M.

"During the first quarter, we produced our highest quarterly loan origination volume in four years, as we continue to see attractive opportunities," said CEO Brian Harris.

"With a strong asset base, robust liquidity, and access to investment-grade capital markets, we are well-positioned to continue growing our balance sheet and earnings," said Harris.

The company originated $621M of new loans during the first quarter, with $372M of loans originated in the second quarter already.

The board authorized a $100M class A share repurchase program on April 21.

An earnings conference call is scheduled for 10:00 AM ET.
